title: Pleroma integerrimum
taxonomy: Pleroma integerrimum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Pleroma, which consists of various species found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The family Myrtaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as the myrtle family. The members of this family are often found in warm and humid climates, with many species being used for their ornamental and medicinal values. Pleroma integerrimum is native to South America, where it grows as a shrub or small tree.
uses: Pleroma integerrimum, a plant species native to the Andes, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties.
